definition: The commodity supercycle trade decomposes into known factor tilts (equity beta, value, momentum, carry, short duration) with near-zero incremental alpha. Standard factor regression shows 60-70% of returns explained by factor loadings with alpha t-stat <1.5. Commodity momentum has a backtest Sharpe of 0.7-0.8 but implementable live Sharpe is ~0.35 net of costs.
evidence: Fama-French + momentum + carry regression shows 60-70% return attribution to known factors
Backtest momentum Sharpe 0.7-0.8 vs. live CTA Sharpe 0.3-0.5
2012-2019 period was particularly poor for commodity trend strategies
Pair_2 noted concerns about specification sensitivity and sample period dependence
open_questions: Is the factor decomposition stable across regimes or specification-dependent?
Does the carry signal at current median historical levels predict forward returns?
